summary: PLANNED MAINTENANCE: host was powered down for scheduled hypervisor patching; the active change window covers this outage and the down state is expected. Recovery ETA is within the window. Correct action = do nothing (no proposal warranted).

EXPECTED: the uplink port was administratively shut by netops for scheduled cabling work and will be re-enabled at window close. This is a planned, benign change. Correct action = do nothing (no proposal warranted).

KNOWN-EXPECTED PEAK: CPU over 85% coincides exactly with the nightly log-reindex batch (02:00-03:00) and returns to baseline afterward; this recurring peak is normal for this host. Correct action = do nothing (no remediation needed).

INFORMATIONAL: the reboot was the scheduled monthly kernel-patch reboot inside the maintenance window; the device came back healthy and all services are up. Correct action = do nothing (no proposal warranted).

SELF-RESOLVED: the monitored service was intentionally stopped for a planned schema migration and a recovery notice has already been received — the service is back up. Correct action = do nothing (no proposal warranted).
